hook: No will means a judge decides what happens to your estate.
body: Searches for 'how to write a last will and testament' are at a high right now — which means a lot of people are realizing they've been putting this off. Your home, your savings, and your wishes deserve better than a courtroom default. Find out exactly where your estate plan stands before life makes the decision for you.

hook: Your parent passes. No will. Now what does your family do?
body: Frozen accounts, family conflict, months in probate court — none of that is inevitable, but it becomes very likely without a plan in place. Most aging parents haven't updated their documents in decades, or never started at all. Knowing what's missing before a crisis hits is the kindest thing you can do for everyone involved.

hook: Who raises your kids if you're both gone? Write it down.
body: Naming a guardian is one of the most important things a parent can do — and one of the most commonly skipped. Without it, a judge makes that call based on legal criteria, not on what you would have chosen. It takes one decision to make sure the right person is named.

hook: Probate is public, slow, and expensive. It's also mostly avoidable.
body: When assets pass through probate, your family waits — sometimes for years — while courts sort out what you owned and who gets it. The right documents and account titling can route most assets around that process entirely. The question is whether yours are set up to do that.

hook: Your parent can't speak. You can't act. Not without this document.
body: A power of attorney gives someone the legal authority to manage finances and make medical decisions when a parent no longer can. Without it, even the most loving family member has no legal standing — and courts get involved fast. This is one of the simplest documents to have in place, and one of the most overlooked.

hook: Your estate plan is only as strong as the money behind it.
body: For families with young kids and a mortgage, life insurance isn't optional — it's what makes every other part of your plan actually work. No coverage means your family may inherit a plan they can't afford to execute. The good news is that finding out where your gaps are takes about 10 minutes.

hook: Trusts aren't just for the wealthy. They're for anyone with a family.
body: A revocable living trust lets your assets pass to the people you choose — without probate, without court delays, and without your financial life becoming public record. With tax and retirement policy shifting, how your estate is structured matters more than ever. Find out if a trust belongs in your plan.
